2606.09433.title: Bayesian Selective Latent Inference for Wastewater-First Influenza Monitoring
2606.09433.paper_summary: Wastewater influenza surveillance can reveal community circulation before clinical reporting, but wastewater alone is not a fully identifiable proxy for human burden. Existing wastewater models assume a fixed evidence set, while generic evidence-acquisition methods treat official surveillance streams as interchangeable costly features. We cast wastewater-first influenza monitoring as a selective decision problem: starting from mandatory wastewater evidence, the system must decide whether wastewater is sufficient, which delayed official stream to query next, and when abstention is the only scientifically defensible action under source ambiguity. We propose Bayesian Selective Latent Inference (BSLI), a principled Bayesian method that maintains a posterior over latent burden and identifiability, certifies answerability through explicit scientific gates, and optimizes query-stop decisions with an exact cost-calibrated Bellman policy. We prove the key variational, answerability, Bellman-optimality, and one-dimensional cost-calibration properties. On a fixed public-data benchmark with 5,933 forecasting episodes and 3,102 source-ambiguity episodes, BSLI improves the matched-budget cost-performance frontier while preserving conservative abstention under source ambiguity.
2606.09433.paper_summary_zh: 廢水流感監測能夠在臨床報告之前揭示社區的流行情況，但僅依賴廢水並不能完全識別人類的負擔。現有的廢水模型假設一組固定的證據，而通用的證據獲取方法則將官方監測流視為可互換的高成本特徵。我們將以廢水為首的流感監測視為一個選擇性決策問題：從強制性的廢水證據開始，系統必須決定廢水是否足夠、接下來查詢哪個延遲的官方流，以及在來源不明的情況下，何時放棄是唯一科學上可辯護的行動。我們提出貝葉斯選擇性潛在推斷（BSLI），這是一種原則性的貝葉斯方法，保持對潛在負擔和可識別性的後驗，通過明確的科學門檻來證明可回答性，並使用精確的成本校準貝爾曼策略來優化查詢停止決策。我們證明了關鍵的變分性、可回答性、貝爾曼最優性和一維成本校準特性。在一個固定的公共數據基準上，包含5,933個預測事件和3,102個來源不明事件，BSLI在保持保守放棄的同時改善了匹配預算的成本效能邊界。
2606.09433.author: Yixuan Zhang et.al.
2606.09433.authors: Yixuan Zhang, Yang Song, Hao Wang, Samir Bhatt, Hengguan Huang
